steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks what Kjær's team would logically suggest given the facts. The key constraint is that the team cannot determine the age of the crater, which means they cannot link it to the beginning of the Younger Dryas.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'it can\'t be concluded that the impact that made the crater was connected to the beginning of the Younger Dryas.': Without knowing the crater's age, the team cannot establish the timing connection that the Younger Dryas debate requires. This follows directly from the passage. ✓
  • Choice B — 'it can\'t be determined whether a comet fragment could make a crater as large as 19 miles wide.': The passage never raises doubt about whether a comet can make a large crater; crater size is not the issue. ✗
  • Choice C — 'scientists have ignored the possibility that something other than a comet fragment could have made the crater.': Nothing in the passage implies the crater's cause is in question; the passage focuses on timing, not origin. ✗
  • Choice D — 'the scientists who believe an impact caused the Younger Dryas have made incorrect assumptions about when the period began.': The passage does not suggest the dating of the Younger Dryas itself is disputed, only the crater's date. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
